threat_of_new_entrants.name: Threat of New Entrants
threat_of_new_entrants.explanation: Extremely high capital requirements for compute infrastructure, need for top-tier AI talent, and complex technical expertise create significant barriers. However, well-funded tech giants can still enter.

bargaining_power_of_suppliers.name: Bargaining Power of Suppliers
bargaining_power_of_suppliers.explanation: Critical dependence on cloud compute providers (AWS, Google Cloud, Azure) and limited GPU suppliers (primarily NVIDIA) creates significant supplier power. Talent market is also highly competitive.

bargaining_power_of_buyers.name: Bargaining Power of Buyers
bargaining_power_of_buyers.explanation: Enterprise customers have multiple LLM options and can switch between providers. However, switching costs exist due to API integration and model-specific optimizations.

threat_of_substitutes.name: Threat of Substitutes
threat_of_substitutes.explanation: While open-source models and traditional software provide alternatives, advanced LLMs have unique capabilities that are difficult to replicate with substitutes for complex tasks.

competitive_rivalry.name: Competitive Rivalry
competitive_rivalry.explanation: Intense competition from well-funded rivals (OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Meta) racing for AI supremacy. Rapid innovation cycles and aggressive pricing strategies characterize the market.
